parameters: Telling myself that I can't just sleep in every single morning, I headed out....
only to find my old man and Shino talking about something.
...You're helping us out a lot... so you really don't...
The relationship between us and the Kurumizawas is a bit different from normal.
A fatherless home, and a motherless one. As neighbours who support each other,
sometimes Shino, the one who takes care of their kitchen, and my father, our
breadwinnder, sometime exchange food expenses and whatnot.
スライドスピード = def
--Of course, I am very grateful that he raised me despite the troubled family environment.
I also realize that I'm the most useless person out of all the people here.
I'm really not on speaking terms with my father.
Your mother is dead because of me.
--Even as the memories dissolved into the past, they still bound
us and our relationship like cursed chains.
Would you like to view [Shino's viewpoint]?
(You can view it at any time from the Room of Recollections.)

アニメーションタイプ = def
Leaning against the railing of the common hallway, Shino
waved her hand in my direction.
\SE[14-nb]What was I doing? I was just spacing out and looking at the scenery~
\SE[11]\SM[slidef]Fufuh--, like for example, there. It's a bit far, can you see it?
Looking at where her finger was pointing....
It's the candy store we used to go to a lot, as kids. We used to take our
tiny allowances and pool them together, and share all kinds of stuff.
...We could never agree on whose favourites to buy, of course.
Fufuh--, I was the stronger one of us back then though, so
you always had to give in.
I smiled wrily as I thought back to just how tyrannical she used to be.
Ah, she's grown up and softened out so much... in more ways than one.
It's the shrine I sneaked into by myself to explore, do you remember?
I was such a brat back then... It's embarrassing to think about...
You searched for me everywhere too, \n[1]. Mom told me
all about it afterwards. Fufuh--... thanks.
...But after onee-chan found me, everyone started making a
fuss because you hadn't come back. It's kind of funny,
Even now, I remember the embarrassment of the moment like it was yesterday.
It was a typical case of going out for wool and coming home shorn.
\se[13-nb]Eheheh, I'm sorry. ...But it made me really happy, you know?
\se[13-nb]It wasn't all happy times, of course.
I'm sure they'll all be wonderful memories to look back at.
\SE[11]...I want to, make lots and lots more memories together.
Her happy, yet melancholic expression captivated my eyes, and took my breath away.
...Our gazes overlapped, though she looked away first.
\SM[double]\se[14-nb]Eheheh, I'm starting to feel a bit sentimental.
Let's see, I think it's about time for me to head home~.
スライドスピード = def
I watched her back as she went inside.

...The day will certainly come, when this scenery will no longer be something we can take 
for granted.
Thinking along those lines, I considered burning it into my eyes while I could.
And so I leaned against the railing, and gazed at the dusk.
